ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Title: Encounter Architect
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Ruleset.lazy.Label: Lazy Benchmark (CR)
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Lazy.BossTooltip: CR {cr} exceeds the single-monster benchmark of {max} — may be a deadly threat on its own.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Lazy.LooseGauge: Treat as a loose gauge — terrain, tactics, waves, rests, and magic items all shift real difficulty.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Lazy.BossNote: A crowned monster's CR is above the single-monster benchmark.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Party.None: No primary party is set. In the Actors sidebar, right-click a Group actor and choose "Make Primary Party."
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Party.Empty: The primary party has no player characters or allied NPCs.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.EmptyHint: Drag NPCs here, search below, or use the compendium browser.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.AddTooltip: Click to add to the encounter
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.ClearConfirm: Remove all monsters from the current encounter?
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Notify.NotNpc: Only NPC actors can be added to an encounter.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Notify.NoBrowser: The dnd5e Compendium Browser is unavailable.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Settings.DefaultRule: Which encounter-building ruleset the tool opens with.
ENCOUNTER_ARCHITECT.Settings.LazyHighPow: At party level 11+, show the optional higher 'deadly' threshold (¾ × total levels, or 1× at 17+).
